RULING - PLAINTIFF WHITNEY LEEMAN'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlements And Consent Judgments As To Defendants R.C. Bigelow, Inc.; Teance Fine Teas; The Hain Celestial Group, Inc.; And Walong Marketing, Inc - At the hearing, the parties and the Attorney General addressed two portions of the AG's objection: 1) "Leeman is ' now bound by that standard." (5:13) and 2) "Whatever merit her claims might have had when she first filed the case and argued for a lower standard, that changed when she agreed to the 10 ppb standard, thereby admitting that she no longer has claims against companies that meet this standard. The Court should dismiss Bigelow, Celestial, and Walong." (11:13-16). The parties addressed whether there is any authority for or against the AG's positions that plaintiff Whitney Leeman is "bound" by the 10ppb standard and this court is empowered to dismiss Bigelow, Celestial, and Walong.
After hearing arguments the court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF WHITNEY LEEMAN'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlements And Consent Judgments As To Defendants R.C. Bigelow, Inc.; Teance Fine Teas; The Hain Celestial Group, Inc.; And Walong Marketing, Inc - The parties to send by way of e-mail further briefing and future hearing date on the consolidated approval of the settlement and OSC why Bigelow and Celestial should not be dismissed. The court likewise approve the settlement with Teance Fine Teas. Moving party to prepare form of order and judgment as to Teance Fine Teas.
